entities.name: SOIL EROSION
entities.description: The wearing away of topsoil by water or wind, identified as a primary problem in the central highlands of Ethiopia

entities.name: MOISTURE STRESS
entities.description: A condition of water deficiency in soil that affects plant growth and productivity

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION
entities.description: A set of practices implemented by the Ethiopian government and communities to reduce soil loss and enhance water availability

entities.name: WATER HARVESTING
entities.description: The process of collecting and storing rainwater for later use to mitigate moisture stress

entities.name: RUNOFF
entities.description: The portion of precipitation that flows over the land surface, measured at plot and watershed scales

entities.name: SEDIMENT YIELD
entities.description: The total amount of soil and organic matter transported from a land area, which is reduced by conservation practices

entities.name: SUSPENDED SEDIMENT YIELD
entities.description: The specific measurement of sediment carried in suspension within runoff water

entities.name: CENTRAL HIGHLANDS OF ETHIOPIA
entities.description: The specific geographic region where the land use and conservation study was conducted

entities.name: EROSION PLOT EXPERIMENTS
entities.description: A monitoring method used to collect daily data on water and sediment yield at a small scale

entities.name: HYDROLOGICAL STATIONS
entities.description: Facilities used to monitor water and sediment yield at a larger watershed scale

entities.name: LAND USE
entities.description: The management and modification of land for various purposes, which impacts runoff and sediment yield

entities.name: PLOT SCALE
entities.description: A small-scale spatial unit used for detailed monitoring of erosion and runoff

entities.name: WATERSHED SCALE
entities.description: A large-scale spatial unit encompassing a drainage basin used to assess the overall impact of conservation measures
